title: raphanus sativus subsp. oleifer
intro: Raphanus sativus subsp. oleifer, also known as rapeseed oil plant, is a subspecies of the radish plant that is primarily cultivated for its oil-rich seeds. The plant's seeds contain a high amount of erucic acid, which was once considered toxic but has been found to have medicinal properties in some contexts, although more research is required to confirm its safety and effectiveness. The seeds of Raphanus sativus subsp. oleifer have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease. The seeds can be prepared as oil, which is used in food and herbal preparations, and they can also be crushed or ground into a powder for use in supplements or as a cooking ingredient.
